title: C Read from Files
content:
### Reading from Files in C

Reading files can be done character-by-character, line-by-line, or formatted.

### File Modes for Reading

| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `"r"` | Open for reading (file must exist) |
| `"rb"` | Open binary file for reading |
| `"r+"` | Open for reading and writing |

### Reading Methods

| Function | Reads | Use Case |
|----------|-------|----------|
| `fgetc(fp)` | Single character | Char-by-char processing |
| `fgets(str, n, fp)` | Line/string (up to n-1 chars) | Line-by-line reading |
| `fscanf(fp, fmt, &var)` | Formatted data | Parsing structured data |
| `fread(buf, size, count, fp)` | Binary data | Binary file reading |

### Check End of File

```c
while (!feof(fp)) {
  // read data
}
```

Or better:

```c
while (fgets(buffer, sizeof(buffer), fp) != NULL) {
  // process line
}
```

---

> **Key Point:** Using `feof()` alone is unreliable. Always check the return value of the read function (NULL for fgets, EOF for fgetc) to detect end-of-file.

quiz.question: Which function reads a line from a file?
